SEARCH
Package jGameLib.ecs.ui2d.utils

Interface CacheableRendererComponent<K>

All Superinterfaces:
HasBoundingBox
All Known Implementing Classes:
RoundedRectRendererComponent
public interface CacheableRendererComponent<K> extends HasBoundingBox

A utility to facilitate caching UIRendererComponents' draw results as images, used for components whose draw method is very expensive. Make sure to turn on GameWindow.enableHardwareAcceleration() for maximum performance when using this class, as it enables images to be cached in video memory.

To use this, extend the interface and implement its methods, then call the static method draw(CacheableRendererComponent, JGraphics) in the component's UIRendererComponent.draw(JGraphics) method. For example, 

 public class ExpensiveRendererComponent extends UIRendererComponent implements CacheableRendererBehavior<Integer> {
     private final int someParameter;

     //...

     @Override
     public void drawToCachedImage(Vec2 size, JGraphics graphics) {
         drawExpensively(size, graphics, someParameter);
     }

     @Override
     public int getCacheKey() {
         return someParameter;
     }

     @Override
     public void draw(JGraphics graphics) {
         CacheableRendererBehavior.draw(this, graphics);
         // You are free to do other operations before and after the call, if you need
     }
 }

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    default Cache<K,Image>
    createCache()
    Supplies a new cache to be used in the image cache.
    static <K> void
    draw(CacheableRendererComponent<K> behavior, JGraphics graphics)
    Draws the component, with caching.
    void
    drawToCachedImage(Vec2 size, JGraphics graphics)
    Draws the component to an image to be cached.
    default Vec2i
    getCacheImageSize()
    If the rendered result is not the same size as the bounding box, you may override this method to change the cached image size.
    K
    getCacheKey()
    Get the key to be used in the image cache.

    Methods inherited from interface jGameLib.ecs.ui2d.rendering.HasBoundingBox

    getBoundingBox, withBoundingBox

  • Method Details

    • drawToCachedImage

      void drawToCachedImage(Vec2 size, JGraphics graphics)
      Draws the component to an image to be cached. Note that you must draw the component centered in the image.
      Parameters:
      size - The size of the image
      graphics - The graphics object. (0, 0) is the center of the image.

    • getCacheKey

      K getCacheKey()
      Get the key to be used in the image cache. When the key matches a key already in the cache, the cached image is used and no new image is generated. Ideally, this should take on a relatively small number of values for caching to be most effective. You do not need to include the bounding box size in this method; the cache image size (which is the same as the bounding box size, unless getCacheImageSize() is overridden) is also included in caching.

    • createCache

      default Cache<K,Image> createCache()
      Supplies a new cache to be used in the image cache. You may override this method if your keys are better suited to a different type of cache than the default, which is backed by a HashMap

    • getCacheImageSize

      default Vec2i getCacheImageSize()
      If the rendered result is not the same size as the bounding box, you may override this method to change the cached image size. By default, this is just the size of the bounding box.

    • draw

      static <K> void draw(CacheableRendererComponent<K> behavior, JGraphics graphics)
      Draws the component, with caching. See CacheableRendererComponent for more details
